narrative_blocks.header: Outlived his wife by thirty-two years
narrative_blocks.body: Samuel Whitman married Cynthia Lucelia Talcott in November 1856 when he was thirty-three and she was thirty-one. They had no children together. Cynthia died in March 1876 at fifty; Samuel lived another thirty-two years to 1908, dying at approximately eighty-five.

narrative_blocks.header: Outlived his wife by thirty-two years
narrative_blocks.body: Samuel Whitman married Cynthia Lucelia Talcott in November 1856 when he was thirty-three and she was thirty-one. They had no children together. Cynthia died in March 1876 at fifty; Samuel lived another thirty-two years to 1908, dying at approximately eighty-five.

narrative_blocks.header: Carried both her mother's name and her stepmother's
narrative_blocks.body: Born July 12, 1825 in West Hartford, Cynthia Lucelia received her name from her father's first wife Cynthia Olcott (who died two years before this Cynthia was born) and her own mother Lucelia Brace. She married Samuel Whitman in November 1856 and lived to age fifty-one.

Cynthia married Samuel Whitman in November 1856 at thirty-one and died in March 1876 at fifty without bearing children. Her sister Elizabeth — who had married John Whitman three years earlier — bore seven Whitman children in the same period. Samuel outlived Cynthia by thirty-two years.

burial.plot_notes: Old Center Cemetery, West Hartford CT. FindAGrave 59404431.
